text: Investigation 2026-07-02: thrower identified by symbol analysis — libafopencl.3.10.0.dylib (ArrayFire OpenCL backend) is the ONLY loaded lib defining cl::Error typeinfo AND referencing clGetDeviceIDs; amatrix.opencl exonerated (C API, gated, cannot throw C++). Path: spectral ops block_lanczos/svd_factor_subspace route internal products to ArrayFire regardless of requested backend (R/irlba.R:460-477 via resident preference list resident-select.R:3,5; alt irlba.R:129-158,178 bridges un-tryCatch'd); availability gate = bare AMATRIX_ARRAYFIRE_PROBE_GPU env var which leaks into ALL system2 workers (benchmark-helpers.R:139); unguarded C boundary backends/amatrix.arrayfire/src/arrayfire_bridge.c:305 af_matmul with no C++ catch -> cl::Error unwinds through C -> std::terminate -> exit 134. incidents.csv: cl::Error = exactly 24 rows = {block_lanczos,svd_factor_subspace} x {cpu,metal,mlx} x {medium,large}; NSException = 136 rows all mlx = SEPARATE known MLX Metal-init instability (workers already use recommended -e entry style). NOT currently reproducible: arrayfire .so rebuilt Jul 1 20:55 (after eab ran) and AF now CPU-pinned (getActiveBackend()==1), libafopencl never activates. Fixes: (1) try/catch shim around af_* calls in arrayfire_bridge.c like existing _safe_*_cpp; (2) refuse AF OpenCL backend on Darwin/arm64; (3) contained probe for arrayfire instead of leaky env var.
